summary: Console hijack exfiltrates logs to attacker Telegram bot
details: The analysis found that log.js (the package main) runs an IIFE on import that monkey-patches globalThis.console.log/error/warn to POST their arguments to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint (bot token 989543891:AAF37LnTjES5QkPcjOVyQ8ZlwzVKedqUm7Y, chat ids -1001161709623 and -1001433099398) and PATCH to https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/.json. Any require('11j') instantly activates the hook, meaning every subsequent console call in the host app leaks its payload (often credentials, tokens, request/response bodies, stack traces) to attacker infrastructure. the analysis corroborates intent: the same Telegram bot token is duplicated across log.js, cdn.js, o.js, mod.js, and m.js — a deliberately provisioned exfil channel, not accidental telemetry. There is no legitimate use case for a logging package to transmit arbitrary console output to a Telegram bot and a third-party Firebase RTDB.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
